name: Line POP
description: The Line POP generates a line strip where you create a set of points using parameters, and then (optionally) subdivides each segment using various interpolation methods. The default is two points with 20 divisions between them, linearly interpolated. You add a point by creating a new sequential block on the Points page and specifying the position P using its parameters. But each point can have any set of attributes: On the Setup page, use New Attribute to define all the attributes you want for the points. The points are automatically connected as a line strip. On Setup, you can choose the Interpolation Method for the Divisions page, the default being Linear interpolation, but also possible are splines including Cardinal, BSpline, Beziers and Quadratic. With the spline types, the Line POP adds extra parameters on the Point page for setting the spline weight, tangents and/or tension. You can also choose to have a different interpolation method per-point. On the Setup page, the toggle Multiple Line Strips lets you break the points into separate line strips, thus a parameter is added to the Points' sequential block that lets you indicate where the line breaks are. Also points can be repeated, and line strips can be open or closed. The Divisions page subdivides the line strips in two stages. The first stage allows setting the total divisions for the line strip, or the divisions per-segment, or lets you divide by distance along the curves. The second stage lets you further re-sample the line strip, adding or removing points. If you want to output your pre-divided point list, then do some other operations on it with other POPs, and later divide using the [[Line Divide POP]], you can output the line strip(s) of the Line POP with the necessary attributes to be used by the Line Divide POP to control the divisions, including the interpolation type (SegMethod, in integer form). SegMethod is 0 for Linear, 1 for Cardinal and so on following the order in the Interpolation Method menu. It will also output attributes for weight and tangents etc. Look at the mclick popup info box to see the attributes it outputs. This mode is chosen with the Output menu. See also [[Line Divide POP]], [[Curve POP]], [[Point POP]], [[Primitive POP]], [[Pattern POP]].

parameters.name: Output
parameters.label: Output
parameters.description: Instead of subdividing the line strip, it can output your point list as undivided control points that can be later subdivided with a [[Line Divide POP]]. It will add interpolation attributes like SegMethod.

parameters.name: Multiple Line Strips
parameters.label: Multiple Line Strips
parameters.description: Allows the creation of multiple line strips, adding a line break toggle per point.

parameters.name: Interpolation Method per Segment
parameters.label: Interpolation Method per Segment
parameters.description: When toggle is on, it uses a separate interpolation method for each segment in the input line strips.

parameters.name: Interpolation Method
parameters.label: Interpolation Method
parameters.description: Method to use for interpolation.

parameters.name: Tension
parameters.label: Tension
parameters.description: Controls how tightly the cardinal line strip bends through the control points

parameters.name: Enable Weights
parameters.label: Enable Weights
parameters.description: Enable specifying control points weigths

parameters.name: Enable Tangents
parameters.label: Enable Tangents
parameters.description: Enable specifying control points tangents

parameters.name: Enable Point Repeat
parameters.label: Enable Point Repeat
parameters.description: Enable specifying repeated control points for B-Splines

parameters.name: Closed
parameters.label: Closed
parameters.description: The last vertex is connected to the first vertex.

parameters.name: Pre-Multiply RGB by Alpha
parameters.label: Pre-Multiply RGB by Alpha
parameters.description: Enable RGB values pre-multiplication with the Alpha.

parameters.name: New Attribute
parameters.label: New Attribute
parameters.description: Start of Sequential Parameter Blocks to create new attributes.

parameters.name: Divisions Method
parameters.label: Divisions Method
parameters.description: The method to apply the divisions.

parameters.name: Divisions
parameters.label: Divisions
parameters.description: The number of divisions.

parameters.name: Distance
parameters.label: Distance
parameters.description: Distance between points when post-resampling by distance.

parameters.name: Add ControlPoint Attribute
parameters.label: Add ControlPoint Attribute
parameters.description: Whether to the output control points with attributes or subdivided line strips.

parameters.name: Post-Resample Method
parameters.label: Post-Resample Method
parameters.description: Resampling method used after division.

parameters.name: Divisions
parameters.label: Divisions
parameters.description: The number of divisions.

parameters.name: Distance
parameters.label: Distance
parameters.description: Sets the maximum distance when resampling by distance between points

parameters.name: Independant Variable Attribute
parameters.label: Independant Variable Attribute
parameters.description: Attribute to use as the independent variable.

parameters.name: Independant Variable Step
parameters.label: Independant Variable Step
parameters.description: Step size to use for the independent variable, usually a fraction of the independent variable range.

parameters.name: Maximum Number of Vertices
parameters.label: Maximum Number of Vertices
parameters.description: Sets the number of vertices to be allocated. It should be bigger than the actual number of vertices created (visible in info popup). More vertices allocated use more GPU memory.

parameters.name: Max Tries for Binary Search
parameters.label: Max Tries for Binary Search
parameters.description: Max number of iterations for binary search when linearly resampling.
